description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, slightly bulging body and flat, circular top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each showing a natural wood grain and some dark stains, giving it a weathered, rustic appearance. Three prominent black metal hoops encircle the barrel, reinforcing its structure and holding the staves tightly together. The top surface appears to be a solid wooden lid, flush with the rim, and there are no visible handles or spouts. The wood shows signs of aging and use, with subtle discolorations and surface imperfections. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage yards, or near taverns and markets.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with black metal hoops and a solid wooden lid, showing signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the barrel with visible wood grain, stains, and all three metal hoops.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the curvature of the staves and the seamless fit of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: Another angled view with similar features, emphasizing the uniformity of the wood and metal bands.
description_view_3: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, with visible wood texture and all three reinforcing hoops.
